avalon.name: Read Team Motives

avalon.description: Notice who consistently proposes or supports teams that give their side the best odds, especially around early Quest 1 and after mission results. Use this to infer hidden loyalties by comparing stated

avalon.name: Challenge Team Approval

avalon.description: Challenge a proposed team approval when the lineup includes players with suspicious voting or quest histories, especially in late-game quests where one fail can decide the match. Use it as a Servant t

avalon.name: Assess Team Alignment
avalon.summary: game=avalon | skill=Assess Team Alignment | effects=position_completed | context=use on tag_position to evaluate quest-two team proposals, read alignment from approvals, and shape a trusted quest-three team toward reliable allies
avalon.description: Judge whether proposed teammates are likely all on the same side by comparing their votes, mission outcomes, and consistency with prior claims. Use it before approving teams in opening and midgame rou

avalon.name: Read Opening Proposal
avalon.summary: game=avalon | skill=read opening proposal | effects=setup_completed | context=use during tag_setup to interpret Player 2's initial team [2,0] for alignment and intent signals
avalon.description: Reads the very first team proposal of the game to spot early signals: who is being trusted, excluded, or trying to control the narrative. Use it in Avalon’s opening round, before any quest results exi

candy_crush.name: Make Immediate Match
candy_crush.summary: game=candy_crush | skill=Make Immediate Match | effects=attack_completed | context=use on tag_attack to make the strongest immediate 3-match for quick points and possible cascades while preserving follow-up options
candy_crush.description: Swap adjacent candies to complete a match immediately, clearing pieces and scoring right away. Use it whenever a simple 3+ match is available, especially early in the board to keep moves efficient and

candy_crush.name: Clear Immediate Match
candy_crush.summary: game=candy_crush | skill=clear_immediate_match | effects=clear_completed | context=use when an immediate 3-match is available to raise score, reduce remaining candies, and improve cascade potential
candy_crush.description: Swap two adjacent candies to complete an immediate 3-in-a-row and clear it. Use this whenever a direct match is available, especially early in the board, to score points and open space for new candies

candy_crush.name: Make Immediate Match
candy_crush.summary: game=candy_crush | skill=Make Immediate Match | effects=execute_completed | context=use when a swap creates an immediate 3-candy row or column match for quick points while preserving future board flexibility
candy_crush.description: Swap two adjacent candies to complete a 3-in-a-row match immediately. Use it when an obvious match is available, especially early in the level, to score points, clear space, and set up cascades.

candy_crush.name: Swap Row 3 Center
candy_crush.summary: game=candy_crush | skill=Swap Row 3 Center | effects=position_completed | context=use when tagging the strongest immediate 3-match by swapping the center candies in row 3
candy_crush.description: Swap the two center candies in row 3 to create an immediate 3-match or trigger a nearby cascade. Use it in midgame when that row’s middle controls multiple possible matches or can activate special can

diplomacy.name: Pressure Russia North

diplomacy.description: Push into Russia’s northern holdings by coordinating pressure on St. Petersburg and nearby seas, often with northern allies while Austria advances elsewhere. Use it when Russia is overextended or tied

diplomacy.name: Fortify the Frontier
diplomacy.summary: game=diplomacy | skill=Fortify the Frontier | effects=build_completed | context=use during build phase to place a defensive unit in Trieste or Budapest, securing key borders and deterring Italy, Turkey, or Russia pressure
diplomacy.description: Use this build when Austria has an open home center after the winter adjustments to strengthen its borders. Add an army in Vienna or Trieste, or a fleet in Trieste, to cover key fronts against Italy,

diplomacy.name: Secure Defensive Line
diplomacy.summary: game=diplomacy | skill=Secure Defensive Line | effects=defend_completed | context=use when defending key centers, choosing safe retreats, and preserving flexible coverage against advancing neighbors without blocking home centers
diplomacy.description: Form a tight Austrian defensive shell—typically holding Vienna/Serbia while fleets anchor Greece/Trieste—to cover home centers and key approaches. Use it when pressured by Italy, Turkey, or Russia, es

diplomacy.name: Secure Balkan Frontier
diplomacy.summary: game=diplomacy | skill=Secure Balkan Frontier | effects=position_completed | context=use when Austria should hold Greece and Serbia, cover Trieste, reinforce Balkans with army/fleet builds, and pressure Russia, Italy, or Turkey from Galicia/Bohemia while maintaining a defensive eastern frontier
diplomacy.description: As Austria, secure the Balkans by taking and holding Serbia, Greece, and Albania while keeping Vienna covered and the Adriatic under control. Use this in 1901–1902 to stabilize your southern frontier

diplomacy.name: Open Serbia Galicia
diplomacy.summary: game=diplomacy | skill=Open Serbia Galicia | effects=setup_completed | context=Use in setup when opening toward Serbia and Galicia while diplomatically containing Italy and Russia
diplomacy.description: Austria opens with A Bud→Ser and A Vie→Gal, usually alongside F Tri→Alb or hold, to claim Serbia while contesting Galicia immediately. Use it when you want early pressure on Russia and a strong Balkan

pokemon_red.name: Win the Battle
pokemon_red.summary: game=pokemon_red | skill=Win the Battle | effects=attack_completed | context=use during any active battle to keep attacking until the opposing Pokemon faints, including rival trainer and wild encounters
pokemon_red.description: Use a damaging move in battle to lower the opposing Pokémon’s HP and eventually knock it out. Use this on your turn whenever a fight starts or continues and you want to win the battle in Pokémon Red.

pokemon_red.name: Heal at Pokecenter
pokemon_red.summary: game=pokemon_red | skill=Heal at Pokecenter | effects=clear_completed | context=use after a clear milestone or battle win to restore HP and PP at a Pokemon Center before continuing the route
pokemon_red.description: Use a Pokémon Center to fully heal your party’s HP and cure status conditions by talking to Nurse Joy and choosing to heal. Do this after battles, before caves/routes, or anytime your team is weakened

pokemon_red.name: Take Starter Pokémon
pokemon_red.summary: game=pokemon_red | skill=Take Starter Pokémon | effects=collect_completed | context=use in Oak's Lab at the start of the game to interact with a Poké Ball and receive your first Pokémon from Professor Oak
pokemon_red.description: Choose your first Pokémon from Professor Oak’s table in Pallet Town after he stops you from leaving town. Use this at the start of Pokémon Red to receive Bulbasaur, Charmander, or Squirtle and begin t

pokemon_red.name: Finish Oak Lab Start
pokemon_red.summary: game=pokemon_red | skill=Finish Oak Lab Start | effects=execute_completed | context=use to advance Oak's Lab opening after choosing and naming Squirtle, talk to Blue, complete the first rival battle, and finish post-battle intro dialogue before leaving
pokemon_red.description: Completes the final Oak’s Lab intro sequence in Pokémon Red: receive your starter, finish the first rival battle, and exit the lab. Use it right after choosing a Pokémon when the opening lab events ar

pokemon_red.name: Explore Opening Area
pokemon_red.summary: game=pokemon_red | skill=Explore Opening Area | effects=explore_completed | context=use when starting in Pallet Town to inspect Oak's Lab, talk to nearby NPCs, check notable objects, and head north on Route 1 toward Viridian City to find the next progression point
pokemon_red.description: Explores the opening routes and towns around Pallet Town/Viridian to reveal tiles, enter buildings, talk to NPCs, and spot items or exits. Use it early in Pokémon Red after gaining control, before com

pokemon_red.name: Start the Journey
pokemon_red.summary: game=pokemon_red | skill=Start the Journey | effects=navigate_completed | context=use when leaving Red's bedroom and house, exiting Oak's Lab, and heading north from Pallet Town toward Viridian City to begin early-game progression
pokemon_red.description: Starts a new Pokémon Red game by advancing through the title screen and opening intro dialogue. Use it at the very beginning, from the startup/title screen, to reach the initial in-game opening sequen

pokemon_red.name: Trigger Position Event
pokemon_red.summary: game=pokemon_red | skill=Trigger Position Event | effects=position_completed | context=use when movement to a specific map location or NPC trigger is required to advance story or start a key event/battle
pokemon_red.description: Stand on the specific map tile or coordinate that triggers a hidden position-based event in Pokémon Red, such as advancing a scripted scene. Use it when you need to activate story progress or a locati

pokemon_red.name: Start with Squirtle
pokemon_red.summary: game=pokemon_red | skill=Start with Squirtle | effects=setup_completed | context=use during opening setup to enter Oak's Lab, choose and nickname Squirtle, receive the Pokédex, and heal at Viridian Pokecenter before heading north
pokemon_red.description: In Pokémon Red’s opening at Professor Oak’s lab, choose Squirtle as your starter from the Poké Ball table. Use this at the beginning of a new game when setting up a run that starts with Squirtle.

pokemon_red.name: Preserve Squirtle Safely
pokemon_red.summary: game=pokemon_red | skill=Preserve Squirtle Safely | effects=survive_completed | context=use in wild battles when Squirtle is low HP to heal immediately, safely finish the fight, or flee while conserving health and resources
pokemon_red.description: Keep Squirtle alive by switching it out before HP gets dangerously low and using safer teammates or items to finish fights. Use this whenever Squirtle is threatened by strong attacks, bad matchups, or

sokoban.name: Open Box Route
sokoban.summary: game=sokoban | skill=Open Box Route | effects=attack_completed | context=use when attacking by pushing a box off a wall or along a column to free its path toward the target area
sokoban.description: Moves the worker around an accessible side of a box to get behind it, opening a valid push route. Use it in Sokoban when a box is reachable but blocked from the needed pushing direction.

sokoban.name: Avoid Deadlock Pushes
sokoban.summary: game=sokoban | skill=Avoid Deadlock Pushes | effects=defend_completed | context=use on tag_defend when a box is deadlocked or near a wall/corner; stop futile pushes, preserve worker mobility, and stay positioned to avoid making the box unsolvable
sokoban.description: Before pushing a box, check that the destination won’t trap it against a wall or in a corner unless that square is a goal. Use this in midgame when repositioning near edges so you preserve future push

sokoban.name: Push Box Onto Target
sokoban.summary: game=sokoban | skill=push box onto target | effects=execute_completed | context=use when a box is aligned for a final push into a target square, especially pushing up or left into an open target
sokoban.description: Push a box one square onto a target by moving the worker behind it and shoving it forward. Use this when a box is lined up with a target and the destination square is open, without trapping the box.

sokoban.name: Probe Corridor Access
sokoban.summary: game=sokoban | skill=Probe Corridor Access | effects=explore_completed | context=use during exploration to test corridor routes toward a trapped box and avoid repeated side-to-side oscillation
sokoban.description: Moves the worker down narrow hallways and dead-end passages to check whether they connect to useful pushing positions or trap routes. Use it when the remaining box’s path is unclear and you need to re

sokoban.name: Reach Push Position
sokoban.summary: game=sokoban | skill=Reach Push Position | effects=navigate_completed | context=navigate around boxes and walls to get behind/below/beside a target box and set up the next push toward the goal
sokoban.description: Move the worker to the exact square beside a box needed for the next push, without moving any boxes yet. Use this in Sokoban when setting up a planned push path, especially early in the level.

sokoban.name: Detect Deadlock Early
sokoban.summary: game=sokoban | skill=detect_deadlock_early | effects=optimize_completed | context=use when a box is irretrievably stuck and the puzzle is unsolvable, to stop oscillating and end with minimal wasted moves
sokoban.description: Detects deadlocks early by checking whether a pushed box is trapped against walls, in corners, or blocking narrow corridors without reaching a goal. Use it before every push, especially in endgames, t

sokoban.name: Get Behind Box
sokoban.summary: game=sokoban | skill=Get Behind Box | effects=position_completed | context=move to the safe pushing side of a box, typically below or beside it, to line up the next push toward a target
sokoban.description: Move the worker around to stand directly behind a box, on the side opposite the direction you want to push. Use this in Sokoban before any push, especially early on when setting up a box’s path.

sokoban.name: Reach Upper-Left Access
sokoban.summary: game=sokoban | skill=Reach Upper-Left Access | effects=setup_completed | context=use during setup to reposition the worker toward the top opening or below the box so the box can be approached from its upper-left side and safely pushed downward
sokoban.description: Move the worker into the upper-left access route by navigating to the left side and then up around the box. Use this when you need top-left positioning to approach or push the box from below or the ri

sokoban.name: Hold Position Safely
sokoban.summary: game=sokoban | skill=Hold Position Safely | effects=survive_completed | context=use when survival is tagged and pushing would cause useless oscillation; make a safe non-push move instead
sokoban.description: Keeps the worker on a safe floor tile without pushing the box into a wall or corner, preserving future moves. Use it in midgame when the path is tight and any push could trap the only box or block acc

super_mario.name: Clear Pipe Cleanly
super_mario.summary: game=super_mario | skill=Clear Pipe Cleanly | effects=clear_completed | context=use when approaching a pipe while moving right; jump over or onto the pipe, avoid nearby Goombas, and preserve momentum to continue safely
super_mario.description: Run through a clear pipe smoothly by entering at full speed and staying aligned so Mario doesn’t bump or lose momentum. Use it when a transparent pipe bridges a gap or leads past enemies to keep the r

super_mario.name: Hit Question Blocks
super_mario.summary: game=super_mario | skill=hit_question_blocks | effects=collect_completed | context=use when a nearby overhead question block appears while moving right; jump to hit it and collect before continuing
super_mario.description: Jump underneath question-mark blocks in Super Mario to hit them with Mario’s head and release their item or coins. Use this when a block is directly above Mario or reachable from a nearby platform.

super_mario.name: Stomp Under Block
super_mario.summary: game=super_mario | skill=Stomp Under Block | effects=defend_completed | context=use when defending against a Goomba beneath a low overhead block; time a safe jump to stomp without hitting the block
super_mario.description: Jump up from directly beneath a question block or brick to hit it from below, making the block bounce and knocking out or disabling enemies standing on top. Use this when a Goomba or Koopa is walking

super_mario.name: Move Right Safely
super_mario.summary: game=super_mario | skill=Move Right Safely | effects=navigate_completed | context=advance right without jumping, line up beneath the nearer question block, stay safe as the Goomba approaches, continue under the block cluster
super_mario.description: Move Mario to the right while staying grounded and avoiding nearby hazards or bad jumps. Use this in the opening stretch or any safe lane to advance toward question blocks, enemies, and the next scree

super_mario.name: Move Under Block
super_mario.summary: game=super_mario | skill=Move Under Block | effects=position_completed | context=move right to align directly beneath the shifted question block for an upcoming jump without jumping yet
super_mario.description: Move Mario so he is directly underneath a question block, lining up his position before jumping to hit it. Use this in the opening area when a nearby block is above Mario and slightly ahead.

tetris.name: Seal and Flatten
tetris.summary: game=tetris | skill=Seal and Flatten | effects=clear_completed | context=use on tag_clear turns to drop into center-right gaps, seal holes, avoid overhangs, and flatten the stack for smooth follow-ups
tetris.description: Place the current piece to complete one or more full rows, clearing them and leaving the surface flatter. Use it whenever you can remove lines without creating overhangs or new holes, especially early

tetris.name: Tuck L Left
tetris.summary: game=tetris | skill=Tuck L Left | effects=defend_completed | context=use on defend when an L piece can fill the left or left-center valley/cavity to avoid new holes, reduce existing holes, and stabilize the stack
tetris.description: Rotate and slide the L piece left to tuck it under an overhang, plugging a left-side cavity without raising the stack. Use it in midgame defense when the board has left-facing gaps and you need to red

tetris.name: Fill S Gap
tetris.summary: game=tetris | skill=Fill S Gap | effects=navigate_completed | context=[NAVIGATE] Drop S into columns 3-5 gap quickly to preserve flexibility for T,Z,I,J
tetris.description: Fill S Gap means guiding an S piece into a stepped notch—two adjacent cells offset by one row—to seal a small zigzag cavity. Use it when your stack leaves that exact S-shaped opening, especially in mi

tetris.name: Smooth the Surface
tetris.summary: game=tetris | skill=Smooth the Surface | effects=flatten contour, reduce holes, avoid overhangs, preserve flexibility | context=use when placing J/S/T to fill valleys or cavities, smooth center or side surfaces, and keep wells and future piece options open
tetris.description: Flatten uneven columns by placing pieces to fill dips and reduce jagged height differences, even if it doesn’t clear lines immediately. Use it in midgame when the stack is moderate and holes exist, so

tetris.name: Place for flatness
tetris.summary: game=tetris | skill=Place for flatness | effects=position_completed | context=use when positioning pieces to flatten the center or left-center surface, fill notches/valleys, avoid creating holes, and preserve flexible future clears
tetris.description: Place pieces to keep the surface as flat and even as possible, avoiding tall spikes or deep wells. Use this on routine placements in Tetris, especially early or when no line clear/setup is available,

tetris.name: Keep Stack Low
tetris.summary: game=tetris | skill=Keep Stack Low | effects=setup_completed | context=setup phase; place L/S/O flat and tuck T into left or left-center notch to smooth the surface, preserve the I slot, and stay flexible for upcoming pieces like O, I, S, L, T, Z
tetris.description: In Tetris, keep the stack flat and low by placing early pieces to fill shallow gaps instead of building towers. Use this in the opening and whenever the board is clean to preserve space and flexibilit

tetris.name: Stabilize the Stack
tetris.summary: game=tetris | skill=stabilize the stack | effects=survive_completed, tag_survive | context=use when survival is priority; place pieces flat or vertically to avoid overhangs, limit new holes, preserve I/T access, cap dangerous columns, and prevent topping out
tetris.description: Use this when the stack is getting messy or hole-filled: place pieces to flatten peaks, cap exposed cavities, and preserve a clean vertical well for future I-pieces. Prioritize safe, low-risk placemen

twenty_forty_eight.name: Clear Safely
twenty_forty_eight.summary: game=twenty_forty_eight | skill=Clear Safely | effects=clear_completed | context=use when needing to open space with safe merges while preserving the anchored high tile and keeping rows flexible
twenty_forty_eight.description: Use this when the board is mostly stable and your highest tile is anchored in a corner: make the safe sweep move that clears a matched pair without breaking your snake/monotonic order. It opens space

twenty_forty_eight.name: Merge Matching Tiles
twenty_forty_eight.summary: game=twenty_forty_eight | skill=Merge Matching Tiles | effects=merge_completed, tag_merge | context=use when adjacent equal tiles can be combined while keeping the board consolidated around an anchored corner tile
twenty_forty_eight.description: Slide the board so two tiles with the same number collide and combine into one higher-value tile, like 2+2=4. Use this whenever matching tiles can merge, especially early on to create space and grow y

twenty_forty_eight.name: Anchor High Tile
twenty_forty_eight.summary: game=twenty_forty_eight | skill=Anchor High Tile | effects=position_completed | context=use when positioning to keep the largest tile fixed in a corner, shift left to preserve open space, align small equal tiles, and set up safe early merges
twenty_forty_eight.description: Keep your highest tile locked in one corner (usually a bottom corner) and avoid moves that pull it away. Use this from the opening onward so lower tiles can stack beside it in descending order for saf

twenty_forty_eight.name: Stabilize Corner Stack
twenty_forty_eight.summary: game=twenty_forty_eight | skill=Stabilize Corner Stack | effects=setup_completed | context=use during setup to keep the highest tile cornered, preserve empty cells, compress safely, and align near-term merges without breaking the anchor
twenty_forty_eight.description: Keep your highest tile locked in a corner and build a descending row/column next to it (e.g., 32-16-8-4), using only the two “safe” directions that preserve that corner. Use this once your corner is e

twenty_forty_eight.name: Preserve Anchor Space
twenty_forty_eight.summary: game=twenty_forty_eight | skill=Preserve Anchor Space | effects=survive_completed | context=use when board space is tight to keep the highest tile anchored in a corner, preserve empty cells near it, and force safe merges to avoid lockup
twenty_forty_eight.description: Keep one corner as your permanent home for the largest tile and preserve an adjacent empty “anchor” cell so the top row/column stays stable. Use this in mid-to-late game, especially when the board is
